Package com.wip.service

Interface CustomerService

All Known Implementing Classes:
CustomerServiceImpl
public interface CustomerService
Service interface defining the business operations for managing customer records.

This interface provides the contract for creating, retrieving, updating, deleting, and searching customer profiles within the courier tracking system. Implementations must enforce role-based access control so that non-admin users can only access and modify their own customer records, while administrators have unrestricted access to all customer data.

Since:
1.0
Version:
1.0
Author:
Rohith Varma K

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    CustomerDto
    addCustomer(CustomerDto customerDto)
    Creates and persists a new customer record using the details from the provided DTO.
    void

    Deletes a customer record by its unique identifier.

    Retrieves all customer records visible to the currently authenticated user.

    CustomerDto
    updateCustomer(Long id, CustomerDto customerDto)
    Updates an existing customer record with the details provided in the DTO.

  • Method Details

    • addCustomer

      CustomerDto addCustomer(CustomerDto customerDto)
      Creates and persists a new customer record using the details from the provided DTO.

      The currently authenticated user is automatically set as the creator of the new customer record.

      Parameters:
      customerDto - the data transfer object containing the new customer's details; must not be null
      Returns:
      a CustomerDto representing the newly created customer, including the server-assigned customerId

    • getAllCustomers

      List<CustomerDto> getAllCustomers()
      Retrieves all customer records visible to the currently authenticated user.

      Administrators receive all customer records. Non-admin users receive only the customers they have created.

      Returns:
      a list of CustomerDto objects; may be empty if no customers exist or are accessible to the current user

    • getCustomerById

      CustomerDto getCustomerById(Long id)
      Retrieves a single customer record by its unique identifier.

      Non-admin users may only retrieve customers that they created; attempts to access another user's customer record will result in a not-found exception.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique ID of the customer to retrieve
      Returns:
      the matching CustomerDto
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no customer with the given ID exists or if the current user does not have access to it

    • updateCustomer

      CustomerDto updateCustomer(Long id, CustomerDto customerDto)
      Updates an existing customer record with the details provided in the DTO.

      Non-admin users may only update customers that they created. The customer's name, email, phone, and address fields are updated.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique ID of the customer to update
      customerDto - the data transfer object containing the updated customer details
      Returns:
      the updated CustomerDto
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no customer with the given ID exists or if the current user does not have access to it

    • deleteCustomer

      void deleteCustomer(Long id)
      Deletes a customer record by its unique identifier.

      This operation is restricted to administrators. Customers with existing parcel bookings cannot be deleted to preserve referential integrity.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique ID of the customer to delete
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the current user is not an administrator, or if the customer has one or more associated parcel bookings
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no customer with the given ID exists

    • search

      List<CustomerDto> search(String keyword)
      Searches for customers matching the given keyword across multiple fields.

      The search is performed against the customer ID, name, phone, email, and address fields in a case-insensitive manner. The result set is scoped to records accessible to the currently authenticated user (all records for admins; own records for users). If the keyword is null or blank, all accessible customers are returned.

      Parameters:
      keyword - the search term to filter customers by; null or blank returns all
      Returns:
      a list of matching CustomerDto objects; may be empty if no matches found
